summary: Lewis Hamilton's arrival at Ferrari injects momentum into a constructors' battle where the Scuderia has wavered, with the seven-time champion's Spanish victory signaling the team's potential to challenge McLaren's recent dominance. Alpine's resurgent midfield challenge collides with Mercedes' need to reassert itself in the constructors' standings, creating a three-way tactical puzzle across Spa's high-speed straights. A Ferrari victory would crystallize Hamilton's stabilizing effect on the team and narrow the gap to McLaren's lead, while a McLaren win would reassert their momentum heading into the season's critical final stretch.
